LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF GRACE CAMPOS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL AND FOR RECONSIDERATION OF SANCTIONS ORDERED ON DECEMBER 15, 2016 IS GRANTED UNDER THE DISCRETIONARY PROVISIONS OF CCP 473. THERE IS A LIBERAL POLICY TO HEAR CASES ON THE MERITS, PLAINTIFF MADE A TIMELY APPLICATION UNDER CCP 473, PLAINTIFF PROVIDES A PROPOSED PLEADING, AND PLAINTIFF DEMONSTRATES EXCUSABLE NEGLECT BASED UPON THE DECLARATION OF LIGIA PARMENTER. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: NEWTON LAM; CLERK: J. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-19-2017 Assigned To Arbitration On Jun-05-17

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Dismissal And For Reconsideration Of Sanctions Ordered On December 13, 2016
Off Calendar Feb-24-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF GRACE CAMPOS' Motion To Set Aside Dismissal And For Reconsideration Of Sanctions Ordered On December 15, 2016 is granted under the discretionary provisions of CCP 473. There is a liberal policy to hear cases on the merits, plaintiff made a timely application under CCP 473, plaintiff provides a proposed pleading, and plaintiff demonstrates excusable neglect based upon the declaration of Ligia Parmenter. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-15-2016 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance by Plaintiff. Plaintiff's counsel is sanctioned $400.00 for failure to appear.
Vivian Lerche, Esq. appearing specially for Defendant.

The Court having reviewed the file finds no good cause or substantial justification for plaintiff's failure to comply with the Court's orders and rules. Therefore, it is hereby ordered that this matter be dismissed pursuant to Government Code 68608(b). The Court finds that a less severe sanction would not be effective due to the history of lack of compliance. The State Bar is to be notified of all the sanction orders as required by Business and Professions Code Section 6086.7 (a)(3).

JUDGE SUZANNE R. BOLANOS; Clerk MOlopernes. Proceedings not reported. (610)
